name: paper-mhc-overview
description: Use web_search to search for 'mHC manifold-constrained hyper-connections paper identity mapping scalability'. Summarize the paper's main contribution in 3 bullet points. Keep your response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer:
- mHC (Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections) by Zhenda Xie et al. (DeepSeek) projects Hyper-Connections' residual mixing matrices onto the Birkhoff polytope (doubly stochastic manifold) using the Sinkhorn-Knopp algorithm, restoring the identity mapping property lost in unconstrained HC.
- This resolves HC's severe training instability, where signal amplification reached 3000x in 27B models, reducing it to approximately 1.6x.
- mHC achieves consistent performance gains across 3B-27B scales (e.g., +2.1% BBH, +2.3% DROP) with only 6.7% training overhead via kernel fusion, selective recomputation, and DualPipe scheduling optimizations.
key_facts: mHC projects HC residual mixing matrices onto the Birkhoff polytope (doubly stochastic manifold) using the Sinkhorn-Knopp algorithm
It restores the identity mapping property that unconstrained Hyper-Connections compromise, reducing worst-case signal amplification from 3000x to approximately 1.6x
Achieves consistent improvements across 3B, 9B, and 27B model scales with only 6.7% additional training overhead
Infrastructure optimizations include kernel fusion via TileLang, selective recomputation, and DualPipe communication overlapping
Published by DeepSeek-AI (Zhenda Xie et al.) on December 31, 2025 as arxiv 2512.24880
